question_text: Match the consumer rights in Column I with their meanings in Column II:

column_b.text: Access to variety of goods at competitive prices
Seeking compensation for unfair trade or defective goods
Protection against hazardous goods and services
Knowledge about quality, quantity, price of goods

explanation: (1) Safety = Protection from hazardous goods (c). (2) Informed = Knowledge about product details (d). (3) Choose = Access to variety (a). (4) Redressal = Seeking compensation (b).
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Safety is about hazards (c), not information (d). Don't swap Safety and Informed.
Choose is about variety/access (a), not compensation (b). Redressal is about compensation.
This completely mixes up all four rights. Safety = hazards (c), not variety (a).

question_text: Match the consumer redressal forums in Column I with their pecuniary jurisdiction (CPA 2019) in Column II:

column_b.text: Above Rs 1 crore up to Rs 10 crore
Above Rs 10 crore
Up to Rs 1 crore
Appeals against National Commission orders

explanation: (1) District = Up to Rs 1 crore (c). (2) State = Rs 1-10 crore (a). (3) National = Above Rs 10 crore (b). (4) Supreme Court = Appeals from National Commission (d).
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: District Commission handles up to Rs 1 crore (c), not Rs 1-10 crore (a).
State handles Rs 1-10 crore (a), not above Rs 10 crore (b). National handles above Rs 10 crore.
District is not above Rs 10 crore (b). It's up to Rs 1 crore (c). This reverses the hierarchy.

question_text: Match the remedies in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: Return of the price paid by the consumer
Repairing the faulty product at the seller's cost
Payment of money for loss or injury suffered
Providing a new product of same type in place of defective one

explanation: (1) Removal of defects = Repairing at seller's cost (b). (2) Replacement = New product in place of defective (d). (3) Refund = Return of price (a). (4) Compensation = Payment for loss/injury (c).
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: Removal of defects is repairing (b), not returning the price (a). Refund is returning the price.
elimination_hints.misconception: Swapping removal of defects with refund

elimination_hints.hint: Replacement is giving a new product (d), not returning money (a). Refund returns money.
Removal of defects is repair (b), not giving a new product (d). Replacement gives a new product.
elimination_hints.misconception: Swapping removal of defects with replacement

question_text: Match the new features of CPA 2019 in Column I with their descriptions in Column II:

column_b.text: Filing consumer complaints electronically
Manufacturer/seller responsible for harm from defective products
Central authority to regulate unfair practices and misleading ads
Alternative dispute resolution for amicable settlement

explanation: (1) CCPA = Central authority for regulation (c). (2) Product Liability = Manufacturer/seller responsible (b). (3) Mediation = Alternative dispute resolution (d). (4) E-filing = Electronic complaints (a).
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: CCPA is a regulatory authority (c), not product liability (b). Don't swap CCPA and Product Liability.
elimination_hints.misconception: Swapping CCPA and Product Liability descriptions

elimination_hints.hint: Mediation is alternative dispute resolution (d), not e-filing (a). Don't swap Mediation and E-filing.
Product Liability is about manufacturer responsibility (b), not dispute resolution (d).

question_text: Match the quality certification marks in Column I with the products they certify in Column II:

column_b.text: Gold and silver jewellery
Fruit and vegetable products
Industrial products like electrical appliances, cement
Agricultural commodities like spices, oils, cereals

explanation: (1) ISI = Industrial products (c). (2) AGMARK = Agricultural commodities (d). (3) Hallmark = Gold/silver jewellery (a). (4) FPO = Fruit/vegetable products (b).
subject: cuet-business-studies

elimination_hints.hint: AGMARK certifies agricultural products (d), not jewellery (a). Hallmark is for jewellery.
ISI is for industrial products (c), not agriculture (d). AGMARK is for agriculture.
Hallmark certifies gold/silver (a), not fruits (b). FPO is for fruit products.
